message:
✨ Add kubeconfig prune mission — safely remove stale contexts (#1301)

Step-by-step mission that:
1. Creates a timestamped backup (~/.kube/config.backup-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS)
2. Tests every context for reachability
3. Shows user what will be removed and asks for confirmation
4. Removes only confirmed stale contexts
5. Verifies the cleaned kubeconfig
6. Links to Cluster Admin dashboard to see results

Includes troubleshooting for accidental removal (restore from backup).

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,124 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kubeconfig-prune",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "Prune Stale Kubeconfig Contexts",
+    "description": "Safely clean up your kubeconfig by removing contexts for clusters that no longer exist. Creates a timestamped backup first, tests each context, shows you exactly what will be removed, and asks for confirmation before making any changes. After pruning, links to the Cluster Admin dashboard to verify results.",
+    "type": "repair",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "estimatedMinutes": 5,
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Back up your kubeconfig",
+        "description": "Create a timestamped backup of your kubeconfig before making any changes.\n\n```bash\nBACKUP_PATH=\"${HOME}/.kube/config.backup-$(date +%Y-%m-%d-%H%M%S)\"\ncp ~/.kube/config \"$BACKUP_PATH\"\necho \"Backup saved to: $BACKUP_PATH\"\nls -la \"$BACKUP_PATH\"\n```\n\n> **IMPORTANT:** Tell the user the exact backup file path so they can restore it if needed.\n> Example: \"Your kubeconfig has been backed up to ~/.kube/config.backup-2026-03-16-214500\"",
+        "commands": [
+          "BACKUP_PATH=\"${HOME}/.kube/config.backup-$(date +%Y-%m-%d-%H%M%S)\" && cp ~/.kube/config \"$BACKUP_PATH\" && echo \"Backup saved to: $BACKUP_PATH\" && ls -la \"$BACKUP_PATH\""
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "List all kubeconfig contexts and test reachability",
+        "description": "Test each context by attempting a lightweight API call. Mark each as reachable or unreachable.\n\n```bash\necho \"Testing all kubeconfig contexts...\"\necho \"\"\nfor ctx in $(kubectl config get-contexts -o name 2>/dev/null); do\n  if kubectl --context=\"$ctx\" get namespaces --no-headers -o name 2>/dev/null | head -1 > /dev/null 2>&1; then\n    echo \"  ✅ $ctx — reachable\"\n  else\n    echo \"  ❌ $ctx — UNREACHABLE (stale)\"\n  fi\ndone\n```\n\n> This tests every context with a 5-second timeout. Unreachable contexts are candidates for removal.",
+        "commands": [
+          "for ctx in $(kubectl config get-contexts -o name 2>/dev/null); do if timeout 5 kubectl --context=\"$ctx\" get namespaces --no-headers -o name 2>/dev/null | head -1 > /dev/null 2>&1; then echo \"  ✅ $ctx — reachable\"; else echo \"  ❌ $ctx — UNREACHABLE\"; fi; done"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Show the user what will be removed and ask for confirmation",
+        "description": "Present the list of stale contexts to the user and ask for explicit confirmation before proceeding.\n\n**IMPORTANT:** Do NOT proceed without user confirmation. Show them:\n1. Which contexts will be removed (only unreachable ones)\n2. Which contexts will be kept (reachable ones)\n3. The backup file location (from step 1)\n4. Ask: \"Shall I remove these stale contexts? (yes/no)\"\n\nIf the user says no, stop here. Their kubeconfig is unchanged.",
+        "commands": []
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Remove stale contexts (only after user confirmation)",
+        "description": "For each unreachable context, remove the context, cluster, and user entries from kubeconfig.\n\n```bash\n# For each stale context:\nkubectl config delete-context <context-name>\nkubectl config delete-cluster <cluster-name>\nkubectl config delete-user <user-name>\n```\n\n> Only remove contexts that the user confirmed. Each `delete-context` also reports what was removed.",
+        "commands": [
+          "# Replace <stale-ctx> with each confirmed stale context name",
+          "kubectl config delete-context <stale-ctx>"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the cleaned kubeconfig",
+        "description": "Show the remaining contexts and confirm the kubeconfig is valid.\n\n```bash\necho \"Remaining contexts:\"\nkubectl config get-contexts\necho \"\"\necho \"Testing current context...\"\nkubectl cluster-info 2>/dev/null || echo \"No active context (expected if all clusters were stale)\"\n```",
+        "commands": [
+          "kubectl config get-contexts",
+          "kubectl cluster-info 2>/dev/null || echo 'No active context'"
+        ]
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Open Cluster Admin dashboard to verify",
+        "description": "Direct the user to the Cluster Admin dashboard to see the updated cluster list.\n\n**Tell the user:**\n\"Your kubeconfig has been pruned. Visit the **Cluster Admin** dashboard to verify the updated cluster list:\"\n\n- Local: http://localhost:8080/cluster-admin\n- Production: https://console.kubestellar.io/cluster-admin\n\n> The stale contexts should no longer appear. If you need to restore, your backup is at the path shown in step 1.",
+        "commands": []
+      }
+    ],
+    "troubleshooting": [
+      {
+        "title": "Accidentally removed a valid context",
+        "description": "**Cause:** A cluster may have been temporarily unreachable during the test.\n\n**Fix:** Restore from the backup created in step 1:\n```bash\ncp ~/.kube/config.backup-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS ~/.kube/config\n```\nReplace the timestamp with the actual backup file name shown in step 1."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "kubectl config delete-context fails",
+        "description": "**Cause:** The context may have already been removed or the name contains special characters.\n\n**Fix:** Try removing it directly:\n```bash\nkubectl config unset contexts.<context-name>\n```"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Your kubeconfig has been pruned of stale contexts. A backup was created before any changes. Visit the Cluster Admin dashboard to verify the updated cluster list.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "kubectl config get-contexts",
+        "kubectl cluster-info"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "kubeconfig",
+      "cleanup",
+      "prune",
+      "stale-contexts",
+      "maintenance",
+      "cluster-admin"
+    ],
+    "platform": "kubernetes",
+    "platformType": "operations",
+    "platformProvider": "KubeStellar",
+    "supportedK8sVersions": [
+      "1.25",
+      "1.26",
+      "1.27",
+      "1.28",
+      "1.29",
+      "1.30",
+      "1.31"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "beginner",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "maintenance",
+      "cleanup"
+    ],
+    "installMethods": [
+      "cli"
+    ],
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "docs": "https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/access-application-cluster/configure-access-multiple-clusters/"
+    },
+    "qualityScore": 97
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.25",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "cloudCLI": "none",
+    "resources": "None — this operates on local kubeconfig only",
+    "description": "kubectl must be installed and ~/.kube/config must exist."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-17T02:00:00.000Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "platform-install-gen-2.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
